title: Clinopodium × thymifolium
taxonomy: Clinopodium × thymifolium is a hybrid species in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Clinopodium, which consists of various species of flowering plants. Clinopodium × thymifolium is known to be a cross between Clinopodium douglasii and Thymus serpyllum, resulting in the unique characteristics of the hybrid. Further classification of the species may involve its order and kingdom, which are likely to be in the Asterids and Plantae respectively. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomic understanding.
uses: Clinopodium × thymifolium has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal remedies to aid in the treatment of these conditions. Its antimicrobial properties may help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Clinopodium × thymifolium is also sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential side effects.
benefits: Clinopodium × thymifolium, also known as wild basil, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. The essential oils present in Clinopodium × thymifolium are primarily composed of monoterpene and sesquiterpene hydrocarbons. parts: Clinopodium × thymifolium, also known as lemon basil thyme or lemon thyme, is a hybrid perennial plant in the mint family. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily the leaves and flowers, which have been used in traditional herbal medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. side_effects: Clinopodium × thymifolium is not well documented, but as a member of the mint family, it may cause some side effects similar to other mint species. intro: Clinopodium × thymifolium, also known as wild bergamot mint or bergamot mint, is a hybrid plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae), characterized by its distinctive minty aroma and compact growth habit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive issues. Clinopodium × thymifolium contains bioactive constituents such as linalool, limonene, and thymol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and topical ointments, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin irritations, and minor wounds.
